sources.notes: Statement and constraints: repeated rerooting operations on a weighted tree; each non-leaf sends its value to the farthest leaf in its rooted subtree.
Used to confirm the two-DFS rerooting implementation: compute farthest leaves, maintain where each value flows, and keep the sum of top k-1 leaf buckets.

reference_candidates.notes: Candidate Luogu solution page; not used as evidence in this review.

annotation.summary: Choose k roots over time to maximize collected value, where after each root choice all non-leaf values flow to the farthest leaf in their rooted subtree.
annotation.constraints: n up to 3e5 over all tests, so all candidate first roots must be evaluated with linear or logarithmic tree rerooting maintenance.
annotation.core_idea: For a fixed first root r, all non-root values deterministically flow to leaves according to the farthest-leaf destination in the tree rooted at r; then the remaining k-1 operations should greedily collect the largest k-1 leaf buckets. Thus the problem is to evaluate every possible first root. A first DFS computes, for each rooted subtree, the best and second-best farthest leaf destinations. A rerooting DFS updates each vertex's current destination as the root moves across an edge. An ordered multiset maintains leaf bucket sums and the sum of the largest k-1 buckets, so each candidate root value can be evaluated during rerooting.
annotation.complexity: O(n log n) per test case.
tricks: after the first operation, greedily take the largest leaf buckets
reroot farthest-leaf destinations with best/second-best child choices
maintain the top k-1 bucket sum dynamically

solution_variants.name: rerooting-topk-leaf-buckets
solution_variants.summary: Compute farthest leaf destinations, reroot them across edges, update bucket sums for moved vertices, and query the top k-1 bucket sum using an ordered multiset.

tags.evidence: The solution evaluates every possible first root by rerooting farthest-leaf destination information across tree edges.
A dynamic ordered multiset maintains leaf bucket sums and the sum of the largest k-1 buckets during rerooting.
The process and rerooting transitions are defined on a tree.
Once the first root is fixed, the remaining operations are optimally spent on the largest k-1 accumulated leaf buckets.
The main difficulty includes carefully updating destinations, bucket sums, and top-k iterator state while rerooting.
